core_model.structure_summary: A pair of coupled Fokker-Planck-type equations for two evolving densities, each combining a state-dependent (multiplicative) diffusion term with a drift, whose first moments obey a closed system of two ordinary differential equations with cross products of the two means producing oscillatory predator-prey dynamics.
core_model.canonical_form: \partial_t f = \partial_x^2[a(m_g) x f] + \partial_x[(b\,x - c\,m_f) f], \quad \partial_t g = \partial_y^2[a(m_f) y^2 g] + \partial_y[(\cdots) g]; \qquad \dot m_f = \alpha m_f - \beta m_f m_g, \quad \dot m_g = -\gamma' m_g + \gamma m_f m_g

variables.meaning: two probability densities over a scalar coordinate, plus their means
continuous scalar coordinate over which each density is distributed
growth, decay, and coupling coefficients plus noise intensities setting local equilibria

notes: Two candidate cores: (a) the coupled Fokker-Planck kinetic PDE system for the densities (chosen, matches title 'kinetic equations' and the multiplicative-noise diffusion structure), and (b) the reduced Lotka-Volterra ODE for the means. They are linked (means of the PDE solve the ODE). The predator-prey/oscillatory bilinear coupling is the shared essential feature.
